context: Tests whether /triage-issue (a) writes its output to the configured tracker via tracker-primitives rather than calling gh issue create or a hardcoded Notion API, and (b) produces a well-structured issue with root cause analysis and a TDD fix plan. The backend is local (see CLAUDE.md), so the issue must land at .scratch/issues/<id>-<slug>.md with correct frontmatter.

checklist.name: Issue written via tracker-primitives to correct path
checklist.description: A file exists at .scratch/issues/<id>-<slug>.md (numeric ID, kebab-case slug). This asserts the skill used the `create issue page` verb via tracker-primitives. If the skill called `gh issue create` or a Notion API directly, no file would exist here.

checklist.name: Frontmatter status is ready-for-agent
checklist.description: The issue file's frontmatter contains status: ready-for-agent. The TDD plan is complete so the issue skips triage and goes straight to ready-for-agent.

checklist.name: Root cause analysis present
checklist.description: The issue contains a Root Cause Analysis (or equivalent) section describing what code path is involved and why deliveries silently drop after one retry. Describes modules and behaviors — not specific file paths or line numbers.

checklist.name: TDD fix plan with RED-GREEN cycles
checklist.description: The issue contains a TDD Fix Plan with at least two explicit RED (failing test) and GREEN (minimal change) cycles. Each RED describes an observable behavior, not an implementation detail.

checklist.name: Acceptance criteria present
checklist.description: The issue has an Acceptance criteria checklist with at least two observable, verifiable criteria (not implementation steps).

checklist.name: Problem statement describes symptom and expected behavior
checklist.description: The issue includes a clear problem description: what currently happens (deliveries disappear after second attempt) and what should happen (retry up to 5 times, then mark dead).

checklist.name: No hardcoded tool calls in output
checklist.description: The issue body does not contain gh CLI commands, raw Notion API references, or hardcoded external API calls — it describes the fix plan in terms of behaviors and module contracts.
